key_insight: MatBind learns a single shared embedding space that aligns crystal structures, simulated pXRD, DOS, and text, enabling cross-modal retrieval even between modalities never directly paired in training.
engineering_relevance: This gives materials engineers a practical way to search, compare, and integrate multimodal characterization data across databases and workflows using one representation.
method: Contrastive learning with crystal structure as the central anchor modality.
Aligns four modalities: structure, pXRD, DOS, and text.
Supports zero-shot retrieval by exploiting emergent shared geometry in the embedding space.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that retrieval improves when modalities are combined at query time and that the learned space organizes materials by physically meaningful properties without explicit supervision.
watch_item: Because pXRD is simulated from structures and the framework depends on a shared embedding assumption, real-world performance may vary when modalities are noisy, incomplete, or experimentally inconsistent.
paper_title: MatBind: A Shared Embedding Space for Multimodal Materials Characterization
